summary: Information sufficiency is now represented in the requirements schema, enforced by generation control-chain validation, checked by pre-design orchestration routing, and preserved by the runtime protocol when missing pre-design facts are discovered later.

implemented_behavior: facts require source/outcome derivation, why_needed, acceptable_evidence, current_status, evidence_ref, and blocking behavior
counterexample_review requires reviewer provenance, checked facts, checked transformations, findings, and pass/approved verdict
blocking facts must be satisfied before the chain is valid
facts must derive from existing source requirements and/or required outcomes
independent counterexample review must cover blocking facts and required information-sufficiency transformations
before-design and before-policy orchestration states route missing sufficiency to RunInformationSufficiencyCheck
pre-goal instructions now require satisfied, derived, evidenced sufficiency before design or execution policy
requirements analysis must derive must-know facts from source requirements and required outcomes
runtime must preserve late-discovered missing pre-design facts as control issues rather than turning them into assumptions or local standards
